top_categories.workout_context: Tour o' the Borders' mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 75 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour o' the Borders' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before September.
52ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Tour o' the Borders, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour o' the Borders. Train it until it feels boring.
75 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Varied gradients on mixed terrain demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Tour o' the Borders, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 3,937ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Tour o' the Borders’s 75 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Tour o' the Borders. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Tour o' the Borders requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 75 miles of mixed terrain in Peebles, Scottish Borders, Scotland.
